pattern: Build a reverse-word lookup map; for each word, probe both its proper prefixes (palindrome suffix) and proper suffixes (palindrome prefix) to find complementary partner indices.
hints: Store each word's reverse in a dictionary mapping reverse→index so you can look up a complement in O(len) time.

For word[i], split it at every boundary: if the left part is a palindrome and the right part's reverse exists in the map (and isn't i), record that pair, and vice-versa for right-is-palindrome plus left-reversed-exists.
The empty-string word is a special case: any palindrome word can pair with it on either side, so check separately for words that are already palindromes.

edge_inputs.description: classic example with multiple bidirectional pairs
empty string pairs with any palindrome word
single word, no valid pairs possible
palindrome word can pair with empty string on either side
word is prefix palindrome of another
all distinct words with no palindrome concatenation
empty string with non-palindrome — no pair
word that is its own reverse plus empty string

stress.description: many short words from limited alphabet to force many palindrome pairs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(2, size)
    seen = set()
    words = []
    chars = 'abc'
    while len(words) < n:
        length = rng.randrange(0, 5)
        w = ''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(length))
        if w not in seen:
            seen.add(w)
            words.append(w)
    return [words]
stress.note: short words over small alphabet maximises palindrome pair density
